TheSecureDukeofFan[樊安公] was[Cao] Jun. He received the succession of his junior uncle,[Cao] Bin, theRespectfulDukeofJi[薊恭公]. In the twenty-second year of theera [217], he was enfeoffed asMarquisofFan[樊侯]. In the twenty-fourth year [219], he died. His son,[Cao] Kang, succeeded him. In the second year of theera [221], he [Cao Jun] was posthumously promoted to the peerage of a duke and given the posthumous title of theDukeofFan[樊公]. In the third year [222],[Cao] Kangshifted fief toDukeofJi[薊公]. In the fourth year [223], he shifted fief toDukeofTunliu[屯留公]. In the first year of theera [237], he died, and was given the posthumous title of theSettledDuke[定公]. His son,[Cao] Chen, succeeded him. During the,, anderas, the fief gradually increased, joining with the previous for a total of one thousand and nine hundred households.
